creator: raimeetravel
caption:
THE coolest things I’ve done around the world!!! It was very hard to make this list and I definitely have a few more that would make the top 10… what are yours?! 

& in less than a month I’ll have something to add to this: checking off my 7th continent when I visit Antarctica 🇦🇶😱! That is, if you consider New Zealand part of Oceania continent because I still haven’t been to Australia 😬 

I’m excited for this year’s travels but I haven’t planned too much yet … so tell me your coolest travel experiences! 

#bucketlist #travelexperience #travel #destinationinspiration #travelplanning

transcription.text: Okay, these are some of the coolest travel experiences I've ever had. Not just places I've visited, but things I've done all over the world. First thing, I saw the Great Migration up close in Serengeti National Park in Tanzania. This is when all the wildebeest crossed the river. It was so cool, I felt like I was in a simulation. Second thing, swimming with humpback whales in Morea, French Polynesia. You can do this from like July until October every year. Next, an overnight small boat cruise through Doubtful Sound in New Zealand. Not a lot of people know about Doubtful Sound, everyone talks about Milford Sound, but Doubtful Sound is so much more remote. I've never heard silence quite like this before. Next was the Summer Solstice Festival at Stonehenge I went to. This was crazy, you could touch the stones. There were thousands of people there playing music, so friendly, there was food. I honestly don't think Stonehenge would be worth seeing unless you see it this way. The next thing is Fête de la Musique in Paris, which is actually on the same day as the Summer Solstice thing at Stonehenge. And I did both of these in the same day. Basically they just have music all over the city, it is so much fun. Next was going to the lesser visited parts of Japan. I feel like it's also an experience because it's so much different than Tokyo, Kyoto, Osaka where all people go. Highly recommend if you're doing a Japan trip, go a little bit off the beaten path. You will thank me later. I definitely need to do a part two, but please tell me your coolest travel experiences because I need some inspo for next year.
